skill.name: patent-landscape
skill.description: Analyze biotech and pharmaceutical patent landscapes to identify opportunities, assess competition, and guide R&D strategy. Provides comprehensive patent analysis and strategic insights for life sciences innovation.
skill.summary: A biotech patent analysis tool that identifies white spaces, tracks competitor patents, and assesses freedom to operate for drug development.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 65 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The static scanner misinterpreted markdown code formatting backticks as Ruby shell execution, patent classification codes and documentation text as cryptographic algorithms, and patent analysis terminology as system reconnaissance. The scripts/main.py file is a harmless mock demonstration script that only uses argparse and print statements. No network calls, file system writes, dangerous operations, or malicious intent were found.

content.user_title: Analyze Biotech Patent Landscapes for R&D Strategy
content.value_statement: Biotech and pharma teams struggle to find innovation opportunities in crowded patent landscapes. This skill provides structured patent analysis, white space identification, and freedom to operate assessments to guide R&D decisions.
actual_capabilities: Search and filter biotech patents by keywords, classification codes, and jurisdictions
Identify white space opportunities in therapeutic areas and technology domains
Track competitor patent portfolios, filing velocity, and geographic coverage
Assess freedom to operate for drug development programs against existing claims
Generate structured patent landscape reports with assumptions and limitations
Analyze partnership patterns and collaboration trends among key players
limitations: Does not connect to live patent databases; relies on user-provided patent data
Cannot provide legal advice or definitive freedom to operate determinations
The Python script provides mock analysis for demonstration purposes only
Patent landscape accuracy depends on completeness and recency of input data

use_cases.title: Biotech R&D Strategy Planning
use_cases.description: Identify underserved disease indications and novel combination therapy opportunities by analyzing patent activity in a therapeutic area.
use_cases.target_user: Research director or scientist evaluating where to invest R&D resources

use_cases.title: Competitor Patent Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track patent filing trends, portfolio size, and technology focus for competing companies in a specific biotech domain.
use_cases.target_user: Competitive intelligence analyst monitoring rival patent activity

use_cases.title: Freedom to Operate Assessment
use_cases.description: Map existing patent claims to a new drug product concept and identify blocking patents, design-around options, and licensing opportunities.
use_cases.target_user: Product development team evaluating a new drug target or modality

prompt_templates.title: Basic Patent Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the patent landscape for [target] in [therapeutic area]. Include patent activity level, key players, and white space opportunities.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a new patent landscape analysis

prompt_templates.title: White Space Identification
prompt_templates.prompt: Identify white space opportunities in [technology domain] for [indications]. Consider underserved disease areas, novel combinations, alternative delivery mechanisms, and geographic gaps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding innovation opportunities in a crowded patent space

prompt_templates.title: Competitor Portfolio Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the patent portfolio of [competitor names] in [focus area]. Compare portfolio size, filing velocity, geographic coverage, and technology focus. Identify partnership patterns.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluating competitive landscape for strategic planning

prompt_templates.title: Freedom to Operate Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess freedom to operate for [product concept] in [jurisdictions]. Identify relevant patent claims, map claims to product features, assess blocking patent validity, and propose design-around options.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluating legal risks before advancing a drug development program

output_examples.input: Analyze the patent landscape for PD-1 inhibitors in oncology
output_examples.output: The patent landscape analysis for PD-1 inhibitors in oncology shows high patent activity across major jurisdictions. Key players include Novartis, Merck, and Bristol-Myers Squibb with dense patent portfolios covering core mechanisms. White space opportunities exist in combination therapies with novel agents and in specific solid tumor indications where patent coverage is thinner. Freedom to operate requires careful claim-by-claim mapping due to numerous blocking patents around the PD-1/PD-L1 axis.

output_examples.input: Compare patent portfolios for CRISPR gene editing companies in the US and Europe
output_examples.output: The CRISPR patent landscape is dominated by the Broad Institute and UC Berkeley foundational patents in the US, with Editas Medicine, CRISPR Therapeutics, and Intellia Therapeutics holding key exclusive licenses. In Europe, the landscape is more fragmented with additional players like Cellectis and Horizon Discovery. Filing velocity remains high in therapeutic applications while dropping in basic research tools. White space exists in delivery technologies, in vivo editing approaches, and specific disease indications outside oncology.

best_practices: Define the therapeutic area and specific target clearly before starting the analysis for more focused results
Include multiple jurisdictions (US, EP, WO, JP, CN) for comprehensive patent landscape coverage
Document all assumptions, input limitations, and scope boundaries in the final report
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for legal patent litigation strategy or infringement analysis without expert legal review
Do not claim exhaustive or definitive patent coverage without verifying the completeness of input data
Do not skip the scope validation step when inputs are incomplete or ambiguous

faq.question: What types of patents can this skill analyze?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on biotech and pharmaceutical patents including therapeutic compounds, biologics, gene therapies, and diagnostic methods. It works with patent data you provide.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to live patent databases?
faq.answer: No, this skill does not connect to live databases. You must provide patent data or search results from USPTO, EPO, WIPO, or other sources.

faq.question: Can this skill provide legal advice on patent infringement?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides structured analysis for research planning. Legal opinions on infringement require a licensed patent attorney.

faq.question: What is white space analysis?
faq.answer: White space analysis identifies areas of low patent density where innovation opportunities may exist, such as underserved disease indications or novel combination approaches.

faq.question: What jurisdictions are typically covered?
faq.answer: The skill supports analysis for USPTO (US), EPO (Europe), WIPO (Global/Global), JPO (Japan), and CNIPA (China) patent data.

faq.question: Does this skill require Python to be installed?
faq.answer: The skill includes a Python demonstration script, but the core analysis workflow works through guided reasoning without requiring script execution.
